1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). 1→"""JSON command harness for CLI commands (input + output + schema). 2→ 3→Adds --json and --schema flags to Click commands with: 4→- JSON error serialization (catches click.ClickException with error_type attribute) 5→- JSON input from stdin (when piped, maps keys to Click params) 6→- emit_json_result() for structured output via to_json_dict() protocol 7→- --schema flag to output JSON Schema for input/output/error shapes 8→ 9→Commands return a result object with to_json_dict() or as a dataclass; 10→the decorator auto-serializes it via emit_json_result() when --json is active. 11→Commands may also call emit_json()/emit_json_result() inline and return None. 12→""" 13→ 14→import dataclasses 15→import json 16→import sys 17→from dataclasses import dataclass 18→from typing import Any, overload 19→ 20→import click 21→ 22→ 23→@dataclass(frozen=True) 24→class JsonCommandMeta: 25→ """Metadata stored on Command objects decorated with @json_command.""" 26→ 27→ exclude_json_input: frozenset[str] 28→ required_json_input: frozenset[str] 29→ output_types: tuple[type, ...] 30→
... [233 lines omitted] ...
52→ """Add --json/--schema flags, JSON input mapping, and JSON error handling to a Click command. 64→ - Any click.ClickException with an error_type attribute is caught and serialized as JSON 65→ - SystemExit and other exceptions pass through unchanged 68→ - Outputs JSON Schema document for the command's input/output/error shapes 96→ json.JSONDecodeError: If stdin contains invalid JSON 97→ ValueError: If stdin JSON is not an object 106→ raise ValueError("JSON input must be an object") 164→ except json.JSONDecodeError as exc: 165→ error_data = { 167→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 170→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data)) 172→ except ValueError as exc: 173→ error_data = { 175→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 178→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data)) 186→ error_data = { 188→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 191→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data)) 202→ error_data = { 204→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 207→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data)) 215→ except click.ClickException as exc: 216→ if not hasattr(exc, "error_type"): 218→ error_data = { 220→ "error_type": exc.error_type, 223→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data)) 248→ TypeError: If result has no to_json_dict() and is not a dataclass 256→ raise TypeError(f"Cannot serialize {type_name}: no to_json_dict() and not a dataclass")

1→"""JSON Schema generation for @json_command Click commands. 2→ 3→Provides: 4→- Click parameter type -> JSON Schema type mapping 5→- Input schema generation from Click command parameters 6→- Output schema generation from result types with json_schema() or plain dataclasses 7→- Full schema document assembly (input + output + error) 8→""" 9→ 10→import dataclasses 11→import types 12→from typing import Any, Union, get_args, get_origin 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_command import JsonCommandMeta 17→ 18→# Internal params injected by the decorator that should never appear in schemas 19→_INTERNAL_PARAMS = frozenset({"json_mode", "schema_mode", "ctx"}) 20→ 21→ERROR_SCHEMA: dict[str, Any] = { 22→ "type": "object", 23→ "properties": { 24→ "success": {"type": "boolean", "const": False}, 25→ "error_type": {"type": "string"}, 26→ "message": {"type": "string"}, 27→ }, 28→ "required": ["success", "error_type", "message"], 29→} 30→
... [231 lines omitted] ...
124→ - Else raise TypeError 154→ raise TypeError( 228→ Returns a dict with command name, input_schema, output_schema, and error_schema. 242→ "error_schema": ERROR_SCHEMA,

1→"""Parity tests: every @mcp_exposed command is discovered and has correct schema.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import asyncio 6→ 7→from erk.cli.cli import cli 8→from erk_mcp.server import _build_json_command_tools, create_mcp 9→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_schema import command_input_schema 10→from erk_shared.agentclick.mcp_exposed import discover_mcp_commands 11→ 12→ 13→def test_every_mcp_exposed_command_is_registered_as_mcp_tool() -> None: 14→ """Every command with _mcp_meta in the Click tree appears as an MCP tool.""" 15→ discovered = discover_mcp_commands(cli) 16→ assert len(discovered) > 0, "No @mcp_exposed commands found — check CLI imports" 17→ 18→ server = create_mcp() 19→ tools = asyncio.run(server.list_tools()) 20→ tool_names = {t.name for t in tools} 21→ 22→ for _cmd, meta in discovered: 23→ assert meta.name in tool_names, f"@mcp_exposed '{meta.name}' not registered as MCP tool" 24→ 25→ 26→def test_mcp_tool_schema_matches_click_derived_schema() -> None: 27→ """Each MCP tool's schema matches command_input_schema(cmd).""" 28→ discovered = discover_mcp_commands(cli) 29→ 30→ built_tools = _build_json_command_tools()
... [38 lines omitted] ...

... [261 lines omitted] ...
45→ def test_nonzero_exit_raises_runtime_error(self, mock_run: patch) -> None: 54→ RuntimeError, 55→ match="erk bad-cmd failed \\(exit 1\\): command not found", 60→ def test_error_message_includes_all_args(self, mock_run: patch) -> None: 65→ stderr=" some error ", 69→ RuntimeError, 70→ match=r"erk exec dash-data --state open failed \(exit 2\): some error", 167→ stdout='{"success": false, "error_type": "auth_required"}', 173→ assert result == '{"success": false, "error_type": "auth_required"}'

1→"""Tests for @json_command decorator, emit_json, emit_json_result, and --schema flag.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→from typing import Any 6→from unittest.mock import patch 7→ 8→import click 9→from click.testing import CliRunner 10→ 11→from erk_shared.agentclick.errors import AgentCliError 12→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_command import ( 13→ JsonCommandMeta, 14→ emit_json, 15→ emit_json_result, 16→ json_command, 17→) 18→ 19→# -- Helpers -- 20→ 21→ 22→def _make_test_command() -> click.Command: 23→ """Create a minimal Click command decorated with @json_command for testing.""" 24→ 25→ @json_command 26→ @click.command("test-cmd") 27→ def test_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 28→ if json_mode: 29→ emit_json({"key": "value"}) 30→ return
... [494 lines omitted] ...
71→def test_catches_agent_cli_error() -> None: 72→ """AgentCliError is serialized as JSON when --json is active.""" 75→ @click.command("error-cmd") 76→ def error_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 77→ raise AgentCliError("something went wrong", error_type="cli_error") 80→ result = runner.invoke(error_cmd, ["--json"]) 84→ assert data["error_type"] == "cli_error" 88→def test_preserves_error_type() -> None: 89→ """Custom error_type is preserved in JSON output.""" 92→ @click.command("typed-error-cmd") 93→ def typed_error_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 94→ raise AgentCliError("auth failed", error_type="auth_required") 97→ result = runner.invoke(typed_error_cmd, ["--json"]) 101→ assert data["error_type"] == "auth_required" 102→ assert data["message"] == "auth failed" 118→def test_error_without_json_flag_uses_normal_handling() -> None: 119→ """Without --json, AgentCliError uses Click's normal error handling.""" 122→ @click.command("normal-error-cmd") 123→ def normal_error_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 124→ raise AgentCliError("normal error", error_type="cli_error") 127→ result = runner.invoke(normal_error_cmd, []) 129→ # Should NOT be JSON — Click's normal error handling 130→ assert "normal error" in result.output 131→ # Verify it's not valid JSON (it's the styled Click error) 138→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 162→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 169→def test_json_input_unknown_keys_error() -> None: 170→ """Unknown keys produce JSON error.""" 185→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json_input" 224→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json_input" 229→ """Missing required field produces JSON error.""" 244→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json_input" 356→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 449→ """--schema outputs a valid schema document with input/output/error schemas.""" 465→ assert "error_schema" in doc 466→ assert doc["error_schema"]["properties"]["success"]["const"] is False

1→"""Core types for provider-agnostic plan storage.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass, field 4→from datetime import datetime 5→from enum import Enum 6→ 7→ 8→class PlanState(Enum): 9→ """State of a plan.""" 10→ 11→ OPEN = "OPEN" 12→ CLOSED = "CLOSED" 13→ 14→ 15→@dataclass(frozen=True) 16→class Plan: 17→ """Provider-agnostic representation of a plan. 18→ 19→ Fields: 20→ plan_identifier: Provider-specific ID as string 21→ (GitHub: "42", Jira: "PROJ-123", Linear: UUID) 22→ title: Plan title 23→ body: Plan body/description 24→ state: Plan state (OPEN or CLOSED) 25→ url: Web URL to view the plan 26→ labels: List of label names 27→ assignees: List of assignee usernames 28→ created_at: Creation timestamp 29→ updated_at: Last update timestamp 30→ metadata: Provider-specific fields (e.g., {"number": 42} for GitHub)

1→"""Decorator and discovery for auto-exposing @json_command CLIs as MCP tools.""" 2→ 3→from collections.abc import Callable 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→ 9→@dataclass(frozen=True) 10→class McpMeta: 11→ """MCP metadata attached to a Click command by @mcp_exposed.""" 12→ 13→ name: str 14→ description: str 15→ 16→ 17→def mcp_exposed(*, name: str, description: str) -> Callable[[click.Command], click.Command]: 18→ """Mark a @json_command for automatic MCP exposure. 19→ 20→ Apply above @json_command in the decorator stack: 21→ 22→ @mcp_exposed(name="one_shot", description="...") 23→ @json_command(...) 24→ @click.command("one-shot") 25→ 26→ Args: 27→ name: MCP tool name 28→ description: MCP tool description 29→ """ 30→
... [31 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Base error class for agent-optimized CLI commands.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→ 6→class AgentCliError(click.ClickException): 7→ """Exception with error_type for JSON serialization by @json_command.""" 8→ 9→ def __init__(self, message: str, *, error_type: str) -> None: 10→ super().__init__(message) 11→ self.message = message 12→ self.error_type = error_type 13→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→"""Tests for @json_command that depend on the erk CLI tree.""" 2→ 3→import types 4→import typing 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→from erk.cli.cli import cli 9→ 10→# -- output_types validation test -- 11→ 12→ 13→def test_output_types_matches_return_annotation() -> None: 14→ """Every @json_command's output_types must match its return annotation.""" 15→ 16→ def _collect_json_commands(group: click.BaseCommand) -> list[click.BaseCommand]: 17→ """Recursively collect all commands with _json_command_meta.""" 18→ result = [] 19→ if hasattr(group, "_json_command_meta"): 20→ result.append(group) 21→ if isinstance(group, click.Group): 22→ for cmd in group.commands.values(): 23→ result.extend(_collect_json_commands(cmd)) 24→ return result 25→ 26→ def _unwrap_return_types(annotation: typing.Any) -> set[type]: 27→ """Decompose A | B unions into member types.""" 28→ origin = typing.get_origin(annotation) 29→ if origin is typing.Union or isinstance(annotation, types.UnionType): 30→ return set(typing.get_args(annotation))
... [35 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Perfect! I now have comprehensive information about all the files requested. Let me provide you with a complete summary of the findings.
## Summary: All Files Found and Read
Here is the complete information about the files you requested:
### 1. `json_command` Decorator Implementation
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_command.py`
This is the main decorator implementation that adds JSON support to Click commands. Key features: - Adds `--json` and `--schema` flags to Click commands - Handles JSON input from stdin (when piped) - Serializes Click exceptions as JSON when they have `error_type` attribute - Provides `emit_json()` and `emit_json_result()` functions for structured output - Auto-serializes command return values as JSON in JSON mode
### 2. `command_input_schema` Function
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_schema.py`
This function generates JSON Schema for a command's input parameters. It: - Reads `_json_command_meta` from the command for exclude/required sets - Skips internal params (json_mode, schema_mode, ctx) - Returns a JSON Schema object with properties and required arrays - Handles Click type mappings (INT, FLOAT, STRING, BOOL, Path, Choice)
### 3. Existing Test Files
**Found all requested test files:**
1. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_mcp_cli_sync.py`** - Tests for MCP tool parity (every @mcp_exposed command is registered as MCP tool)
2. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-mcp/tests/test_server.py`** - Tests for erk_mcp.server MCP tools and _run_erk wrapper, including: - `TestRunErk` - subprocess wrapper tests - `TestPlanList` - plan_list MCP tool tests - `TestPlanView` - plan_view MCP tool tests - `TestRunErkJson` - JSON subprocess wrapper tests - `TestJsonCommandTool` - dynamic MCP tool tests (filtering None values, keeping false booleans, etc.) - `TestCreateMcp` - create_mcp() factory function tests
3. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/agentclick/test_json_command.py`** - Comprehensive tests for @json_command decorator including: - JSON mode functionality - JSON input from stdin - Error handling and serialization - emit_json_result with to_json_dict and plain dataclasses - --schema flag behavior
4. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py`** - Tests that depend on the erk CLI tree to verify output_types matches return annotations
5. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py`** - Integration tests for the one-shot command with JSON output/input
### 4. Additional json_command Test Files Found
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/agentclick/test_json_schema.py`** - Comprehensive tests for JSON Schema generation including: - Click parameter type to schema conversion - command_input_schema generation - output_type_schema for different result types - dataclass_to_json_schema auto-generation - build_schema_document structure
### 5. `Plan` Dataclass
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/types.py`
The Plan dataclass is a frozen dataclass with the following fields: - `plan_identifier`: Provider-specific ID as string - `title`: Plan title - `body`: Plan body/description - `state`: Plan state (OPEN or CLOSED via PlanState enum) - `url`: Web URL to view the plan - `labels`: List of label names - `assignees`: List of assignee usernames - `created_at`: Creation timestamp - `updated_at`: Last update timestamp - `metadata`: Provider-specific fields - `objective_id`: Parent objective issue number or None - `header_fields`: Parsed plan-header metadata fields (dict with default_factory)
### Additional Files Read
**Supporting files:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/mcp_exposed.py` - Decorator and discovery for auto-exposing @json_command CLIs as MCP tools - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/errors.py` - AgentCliError base class for JSON serialization - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_schema.py` - JSON Schema generation functions
All files have been successfully read and their contents are provided above.
